desc: Sidi Ifni is an Atlantic edge town where Spanish-era Art Deco, Amazigh-Saharan influences, and long ocean horizons create a laid-back counterpoint to Morocco’s imperial-city intensity. The draw is slow coastal living—surf, seafood, sea-cliff walks, and road-trip access to wild beaches rather than big-resort polish.
hls: Distinctive former Spanish enclave character with preserved Art Deco lines and sea-facing plazas
Access to surf and dramatic beaches around Legzira, Mirleft, and Plage Blanche
Calmer, lower-cost base than major Moroccan resort corridors
Great Atlantic road-trip stop with mild winters and strong sunset culture

todo.name: Art Deco quarter + former Spanish administrative core
todo.desc: Walk key façades and civic buildings to read the town’s 20th-century urban history.

todo.name: Legzira beach arches at low tide
todo.desc: Time your visit around tide charts for safer access and longer beach walking windows.

todo.name: Ifni fish market and grilled seafood lunch
todo.desc: Go early to see landings, then eat at simple local grills serving same-day catch.

todo.name: Mirleft surf lesson or guided spot check
todo.desc: Choose beach breaks matched to your level; conditions shift quickly with swell and wind.

todo.name: Plage Blanche 4x4 day trip
todo.desc: Travel with a driver-guide due remoteness, soft sand sections, and sparse services.

todo.name: Tiznit silver souk half-day excursion
todo.desc: Add inland craft shopping and medina walls for cultural contrast to the coast.

wifi.note: Generally fine in town riads; weaker in outer beach properties.

prac.fltNote: Sidi Ifni has no practical direct UK/Ireland access; most travelers fly into Agadir (AGA) and continue 2.5-3.5 hours by road. Marrakech (RAK) is a viable backup with a much longer drive, while Guelmim (GLN) has limited domestic/regional reliability. UK/Ireland fare pressure is usually highest in July-August and Christmas/New Year, with shoulder-season competition improving in spring and autumn.
prac.currency: Morocco local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Atlantic swell and rip currents can be severe on exposed beaches; swim only where local safety conditions are clearly acceptable.
Wind can intensify quickly in the afternoon—plan beach walks, drone use, and surf lessons for earlier windows.
Bus frequencies are thinner outside peak daytime periods; book onward seats in advance during holidays.
ATMs and card acceptance are less consistent than in major Moroccan hubs, so carry backup cash for fuel, taxis, and small eateries.

sweet: Oct-Apr for mild temperatures and lower heat stress than inland Morocco.
